findings.message: The new create_item route opens a database session directly via SessionLocal() and performs ORM persistence (Item(...), session.add) inline, bypassing the repository layer. A repository function create_item(session, name) already exists in src/demo/db/repository.py and is the established data-access seam (the GET route correctly delegates to list_items). This route reaches across the route->repository boundary straight into the DB/session machinery, duplicating the data layer and inverting the layering the rest of the module follows.
findings.suggestion: Delegate persistence to the repository: depend on get_session (SessionDep) like get_items does, and call repository.create_item(session, payload.name) instead of constructing SessionLocal/Item and calling session.add here. Drop the SessionLocal import from the route. This keeps the route in the presentation layer and confines session/ORM handling to the repository (which also already commits, unlike this handler).
